winterisms.config.limitsGrapple.hitColor: Color that shows on your Grappling HUD when you're able to grapple.
winterisms.config.limitsGrapple.missColo: Color that shows on your Grappling HUD when you're not able to grapple.
winterisms.config.flashbackReplayForceAl: Warning: Don't touch this unless you know what you're doing. Flashback disables this for a very good reason. If you do know what to do, enter the following:
I understand what I am doing will cause potential instabilities with Flashback. I will not report this to Flashback support. Praise Winter. Praise Moulberry. Praise Ishland.
winterisms.config.incompatibleModsWarnin: If viewed once, will be switched on implying that the screen has been seen. If you want to see it again, switch it off, and then back out of this menu.
winterisms.flashback.incompatibleWithRec:
§cYou have mods which are known to cause issues when viewing replays!§r
If you know §lexactly§r what you're doing, then go back to the Main Menu screen, Mods, open the Config for Winterisms, and enable the Flashback Force Allow Incompatible Mods in order to be able to view replays.

Otherwise, please close the game, go to Prism Launcher, click edit, mods, and then click the checkbox next to the following mods in order to disable the following mods. You'll be able to view replays after.
winterisms.screen.initialIncompatibleWar: Winterisms - First Boot - Potential Problem Mods found!

By default, the 2 overlays will overlap over each other and make StarHUD a little less readable. It is recommended that you go to StarHUD's settings and turn off the Targeted HUD, and go to Jade's config and move down its Overlay Position.

You might have issues, or even crashes trying to view replays, as Flashback and C2ME are incompatible with each other due to how C2ME's chunks work. A config option is provided in Winterisms' in-game config to bypass the check, but uh, here be dragons.
Even if you don't use shaders, you should have Iris installed anyway, as it provides Entity Batching, a major optimization in itself that isn't provided by Sodium, and will always run.
Centered Crosshair doesn't fix all centering inconsistencies around the crosshair, only the crosshair itself, so anything depending on the default centering will look wrong.
